# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Rails mailer class that sends emails via SendGrid. Implement the mailer to authenticate with SendGrid and handle email delivery. The implementation should work across development and production environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

class SendgridMailer < ApplicationMailer
  default from: 'no-reply@yourdomain.com' # Replace with your default 'from' email

  # Example mailer method
  def welcome_email(user)
    @user = user
    @greeting = "Hi"

    mail(to: @user.email, subject: 'Welcome to Our App!')
  end

  # You can add more mailer methods here
  # def another_email(recipient_email, subject_text, body_content)
  #   @body_content = body_content
  #   mail(to: recipient_email, subject: subject_text)
  # end
end

# Configuration for config/environments/production.rb
# config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :smtp
# config.action_mailer.smtp_settings = {
#   address:              'smtp.sendgrid.net',
#   port:                 587,
#   authentication:       :plain,
#   user_name:            'apikey',
#   password:             ENV['SENDGRID_API_KEY'], # Ensure this environment variable is set
#   domain:               ENV['SENDGRID_DOMAIN'] || 'yourdomain.com', # Replace with your actual domain or use an ENV var
#   enable_starttls_auto: true
# }
# config.action_mailer.raise_delivery_errors = false # Set to true in development/test if you want to catch errors immediately

# Configuration for config/environments/development.rb
# config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :smtp
# config.action_mailer.smtp_settings = {
#   address:              'smtp.sendgrid.net',
#   port:                 587,
#   authentication:       :plain,
#   user_name:            'apikey',
#   password:             ENV['SENDGRID_API_KEY'], # Ensure this environment variable is set in your .env or similar
#   domain:               'localhost:3000', # Your development domain
#   enable_starttls_auto: true
# }
# config.action_mailer.raise_delivery_errors = true # Good for development to see errors
# config.action_mailer.perform_caching = false # Or true if you use mailer caching
# config.action_mailer.perform_deliveries = true # Set to false if you want to prevent sending emails in development

# To use it in your application, for example, after a user signs up:
# SendgridMailer.welcome_email(@user).deliver_later

# For email templates, create a file at app/views/sendgrid_mailer/welcome_email.html.erb
# <h1>Welcome, <%= @user.name %>!</h1>
# <p>Thank you for joining our app.</p>
# <p>Your greeting: <%= @greeting %></p>
#
# And optionally for plain text: app/views/sendgrid_mailer/welcome_email.text.erb
# Welcome, <%= @user.name %>!
# Thank you for joining our app.
# Your greeting: <%= @greeting %>
